description_long: This object is a long, segmented crustacean resembling a centipede or millipede, with a series of articulated body segments that curve in a natural, lifelike manner. Each segment is dark brown with lighter brown highlights, and the body tapers towards the head and tail. Numerous thin, pointed legs protrude from each segment, giving the creature a spiny, bristled appearance. The head is slightly more pronounced, featuring two long, thin antennae extending forward. The surface appears hard and slightly glossy, typical of an exoskeleton. The overall form is sinuous and flexible, suggesting agility and movement.
description: A long, segmented, dark brown crustacean with many thin legs and prominent antennae, featuring a hard, glossy exoskeleton.
description_view_0: The side view highlights the length, segmentation, and the numerous legs along the body, with the head and antennae clearly visible.
description_view_1: This angled view emphasizes the curvature of the body and the spiny legs, with the antennae extending forward.
description_view_2: The top-down view shows the full length and segmentation, with the legs splayed outward and the body’s tapering shape.
description_view_3: The rear view displays the tail end and the arrangement of the legs, with the body’s curve and segmentation most prominent.
